ks1.objective: Run with your ball in hand and bounce it on the side of an upside-down cone to make it 'pop'.
successCriteria: Holds the ball securely while running
Aims the bounce at the cone

lks2.objective: Combine dribbling and ball-pickup to flip cones — develop hand-foot transitions.
successCriteria: Flicks or picks the ball up cleanly
Beats their own score across rounds

uks2.objective: Add skills and tricks between cones, and use the cones as targets to flip with a kicked ball.
successCriteria: Uses a trick or turn between cones
Kicks accurately to flip the cone

space.description: A large area with a lot of cones spread upside-down across it. The children start with a ball in their hands.

steps: Children run around the area with the football in their hands.
Every time they reach an upside-down cone, they try to bounce the football on the side of the cone to make it 'pop'.
The aim is to flip the cone the right way up.
Continue until all (or most) cones are flipped.
progressions: Dribble between the cones before picking or flicking the football up and bouncing it on the cones.
Time the group to see how quickly they can flip all the cones over — beat the score each round.
Work in pairs: dribble around the area and kick the football at the cone to flip it.
Friendly count of 'pops' per child — shout out scores at the end of each round.
questions: What part of the cone gives the best bounce?
How do you control the ball as you run with it?
What's the quickest way to flip a cone — bounce, kick or pick up and place?

step.space: Use the whole pitch for top performers; smaller area for younger children
step.task: Challenge top performers with different tricks or skills between the cones
step.equipment: Vary cone sizes — bigger ones are easier to flip
step.people: Top performers complete pair tasks solo

base.showmanship: Talk to the children in front of the parents so they can hear all the brilliant information you're giving. Use the 'Popcorn' metaphor — kernels don't all pop at the same time, so don't compare your child to others. Their turn is coming.
